comment: Colorado ADMT Act (SB 26-189, eff. 2027-01-01): disclosure + appeal + data-correction duties when automated decision-making contributes to an adverse credit decision. The substantive duties execute in the adverse_action node via states-index admt_adverse_artifact flags; this informational rule surfaces the obligation in the rules table so underwriters see it pre-decision.

rules.description: Colorado ADMT: if this application is declined, the borrower is entitled to a plain-language explanation within 30 days, a human-review request path, and correction of inaccurate personal data used in the decision (system generates these artifacts automatically on decline)
rules.citation: Colorado SB 26-189 (Automated Decision-Making Technology Act, eff. 2027-01-01)

artifact_always.text_template: Colorado ADMT duties apply: on any adverse decision, deliver the plain-language explanation, human-review request path, and data-correction path with the adverse-action notice.
